title: Flaky Spicy Dutch Pork Burger
description: A midnight-craving Dutch-inspired pork burger with flaky, spicy layers, pineapple chunks, and high-protein fusion.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine ground pork, panko breadcrumbs, pineapple chunks, onion, garlic powder, red chili flakes, salt, and pepper.
Add the egg, soy sauce, and lime juice; mix until fully blended, but avoid overworking the meat.
Shape the mixture into patties, about 1.5 cm thick; cover and refrigerate for 20 minutes to set.
Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat; add patties.
Cook each side for 3‑4 minutes until browned and crispy; internal temperature should reach 71°C (160°F).
While patties finish, gently fold a small amount of butter into the skillet and swirl to add a flaky finish.
Place patties on a plate lined with paper towels to drain excess oil.
Toast burger buns lightly in the same skillet for 30 seconds per side.
Assemble: spread mustard or yogurt sauce on buns, place patty, top with pineapple slices, a drizzle of lime-infused mayo, and fresh cilantro.
Serve immediately, optionally with a side of cucumber salad.
